question_text: When a gas is heated in a sealed container, what happens to the gas pressure?

options.label: A
options.text: The pressure decreases because the gas expands

options.label: B
options.text: The pressure stays the same because the container is sealed

options.label: C
options.text: The pressure increases because the particles move faster and collide more often

options.label: D
options.text: The pressure increases because the gas becomes denser

explanation: When gas is heated, its particles gain more energy and move faster. These faster-moving particles collide with the container walls more frequently and with greater force, causing the pressure to increase.
